lyrics:
[Intro]
(Fingerpicked guitar, 4 bars — let notes ring, warm and intimate)

[Verse 1]
The porch light's pulling moths against the screen
Your mother's singing something only summer means
The kids are catching fireflies in mason jars
And the yard feels bigger underneath the stars
I got dirt on my hands and peace in my chest
And this whole spinning world has come to rest

[Chorus]
Let the good stay burning
Let the reel keep turning
We're alive inside a moment that the years will ache to hold
Let the porch light flicker
Let the dark come slower
I'm standing in the center of the best thing that I know
And the good's still burning gold

[Verse 2]
Your daughter's got your grandma's crooked grin
She's spinning in the grass like a prayer on the wind
The radio's playing from the open truck
And every note sounds holy, every chord sounds struck
I wanna press my thumb into the face of the clock
And tell the hours you can go ahead and stop

[Chorus]
Let the good stay burning
Let the reel keep turning
We're alive inside a moment that the years will ache to hold
Let the porch light flicker
Let the dark come slower
I'm standing in the center of the best thing that I know
And the good's still burning gold

[Bridge]
One day I'll close my eyes and I'll be right back here
The smoke, the laughter, and the sound of no fear
And I'll tell the ones I love what I should've said then:
That I saw it — every bit of it — amen

[Outro]
So let the good stay burning
Let the reel keep turning
We were golden, we were golden
And the good's still burning
(softly)
And the good's still burning
vocal_style: Raw aching bluegrass tenor with soulful warmth, husky and grainy on verses like Dan Tyminski in quiet moments, opening into round chest-voice soulfulness like Aloe Blacc on choruses. Natural vibrato on sustained notes, slight Appalachian nasal resonance, intimate and unhurried phrasing with conversational breath. Gentle vocal cracks allowed on emotional peaks. Close-mic'd warmth, no pitch correction, deeply human and earnest.

additional_params.voice_character: Male low-mid tenor, warm and grainy with bluegrass Appalachian resonance blended with gospel-soul chest tone. Husky and conversational in verses, fuller and rounder with gentle vibrato in choruses. Emotionally raw and nearly whispering in the bridge. Voice of a weathered but grateful man in his late 30s to 40s — sounds like woodsmoke, worn leather, and firelight.
additional_params.harmony_style: Intimate 2-3 part family choir harmonies entering on chorus second line, close-stacked thirds and fifths, slightly imperfect timing for warmth and humanity. No polished Nashville vocal production — think porch-singing relatives. Harmonies fuller on Chorus 2 than Chorus 1. Bridge is completely unharmonized except a single unison voice joining on the word 'amen.' Outro has close two-part harmony on 'we were golden' fading to solo voice on the final whispered line.
additional_params.instrumentation_context: Acoustic fingerpicked guitar (Travis-style) on verses and bridge, open folk strum on choruses. Bass walk A-G#-F# drives verse harmonic movement. Sparse arrangement — guitar-forward with subtle bass and possible soft kick entering Verse 2. Bridge is bare voice and fingerpicking only. Outro ritardando final 4 bars.
additional_params.dynamic_arc: pp(intro) → mp(verse1) → mf(pre-chorus) → f(chorus1, held back 20%) → mp(verse2, warmer) → mf+(pre-chorus2) → ff(chorus2, full peak) → pp(bridge, stripped bare) → mp→mf→p→pp(outro, gradual fade to whisper)
additional_params.production_feel: Golden hour Americana — warm, analog, close-mic'd intimacy. Jason Isbell meets Aloe Blacc around a campfire. Room tone present. No overproduction. The imperfections are the beauty.
